jokes.text: Wasn't no traffic. It was Carlton's driving. It was old people in walkers passing us.
jokes.setup: Vivian mentions traffic jams causing delays
jokes.punchline: Reveals Carlton drives so slowly that elderly people with mobility aids are faster
jokes.explanation: Strong character-based comedy that establishes Carlton's overly cautious driving. The visual of walkers passing a car is absurd and well-constructed.

jokes.text: Carlton, please, George Burns flipped you the bird.
jokes.setup: Carlton defends his safety-conscious driving
jokes.punchline: Will claims even elderly comedian George Burns was frustrated enough to give Carlton the finger
jokes.explanation: Escalates the driving joke with specific celebrity reference. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: He was waving. The man has arthritis.
jokes.setup: Will claims George Burns flipped Carlton off
jokes.punchline: Carlton earnestly misinterprets the gesture as a friendly wave due to arthritis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - naive and earnest. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: Hey, look, don't feel bad, Aunt Viv. At least you have two normal daughters.
jokes.setup: Will tries to console Vivian about her children
jokes.punchline: Implies Hillary is abnormal, setting up for the next scene
jokes.explanation: Sets up Hillary's entrance but the joke is fairly predictable given her character.

jokes.text: Like I'm gonna learn how to cook just for her wedding.
jokes.setup: Hillary complains about being asked to handle rice at a wedding
jokes.punchline: Reveals she thinks handling wedding rice means cooking it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Hillary moment - perfectly captures her obliviousness. The misunderstanding is both absurd and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Aunt Viv, don't feel bad. At least you have one normal daughter.
jokes.setup: Will previously said she had two normal daughters
jokes.punchline: Revises down to one after Hillary's rice comment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback structure that escalates the running gag about Vivian's daughters.

jokes.text: Aunt Viv, I'd say it's about time you started feeling bad.
jokes.setup: Pattern of Will consoling Vivian about her daughters
jokes.punchline: Final escalation - gives up on consoling entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect capstone to the running gag. The structure builds beautifully across three beats.

jokes.text: Is Linda Blair down there?
jokes.setup: Strange noises coming from downstairs
jokes.punchline: References The Exorcist actress, implying demonic possession-level disturbance
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but somewhat predictable for the setup. Not particularly character-specific.

jokes.text: Yo, take 1,000 aspirin, drink 50 gallons of water... and stay in bed until Carlton's voice changes.
jokes.setup: Ashley appears to have a fever
jokes.punchline: Suggests she'll be sick for a very long time since Carlton's voice may never change
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor playing on Carlton's high voice. The escalation from normal remedies to impossible timeline works well.

jokes.text: tongue.
jokes.setup: Philip talks about loving kids but sometimes wanting to pull out your...
jokes.punchline: Audience expects 'hair' but he says 'tongue' after a pause
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke with good timing. The pause sells the unexpected word choice.

jokes.text: The key is a 60-watt light bulb.
jokes.setup: Will catches Ashley faking fever with thermometer over light bulb
jokes.punchline: Instead of discouraging her, gives technical advice on better faking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment - subverts expectation of being responsible by giving criminal advice instead.

jokes.text: Why? Did she die?
jokes.setup: Will says Paula won't be beating Ashley up
jokes.punchline: Ashley's innocent hope that the bully might be dead
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from the youngest character. The deadpan delivery and economy make it work.

jokes.text: Word up. Carlton getting on your nerves, too?
jokes.setup: Ashley asks how to kick someone's butt
jokes.punchline: Will immediately assumes it's about Carlton, showing how annoying Carlton is
jokes.explanation: Good character-based assumption that plays on established dynamics. Shows Will's immediate readiness to help fight Carlton.

jokes.text: She won't buy anything from me, Will.
jokes.setup: Will explains 'selling woof tickets' (making threats)
jokes.punchline: Ashley thinks it's literal sales transaction
jokes.explanation: Nice misunderstanding based on age and cultural gap. Shows Ashley's innocence.

jokes.text: I will hit you so hard, it will bruise your great-grandfather badly.
jokes.setup: Ashley attempts Will's threat about hitting someone's grandpa
jokes.punchline: Gets the concept wrong - talks about bruising instead of creating a lump
jokes.explanation: Shows Ashley's attempt to be tough but her polite nature ruins the threat. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: That would be great if you were rumbling Princess Di. But, no...
jokes.setup: Ashley delivers overly polite threat
jokes.punchline: Will notes it would only work against British royalty
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference that highlights the absurdity of Ashley's polite threats.

jokes.text: Hey, sorry, man. Homegirl says she was a Vanilla Ice fan. I kind of lost my head.
jokes.setup: Philip asks what's going on after seeing Will's fighting demonstration
jokes.punchline: Will pretends Ashley's musical taste triggered his violent reaction
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection but relies on easy pop culture target. Not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You expect me to believe that? Everybody likes Vanilla Ice.
jokes.setup: Will's excuse about Vanilla Ice
jokes.punchline: Philip seriously accepts this as plausible, showing he's out of touch
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Philip showing his disconnect from youth culture. The earnestness sells it.

jokes.text: Now I have found that as long as you pay them promptly... they pretty much leave you alone.
jokes.setup: Carlton lectures about dealing with bullies non-violently
jokes.punchline: Reveals he just pays bullies protection money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - tries to sound wise but reveals cowardly solution. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: But never forget, Mama Said Knock You Out. LL Cool J.
jokes.setup: Carlton quotes JFK about negotiation
jokes.punchline: Will counters with rap lyric and artist credit in same format
jokes.explanation: Great cultural contrast between characters. The parallel structure makes it work well.

jokes.text: And, Ashley, whatever happens tomorrow... know that I believe Carlton is adopted.
jokes.setup: Will starts inspirational speech like Carlton
jokes.punchline: Ends with insult about Carlton instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts the inspirational moment. Typical Will behavior.

jokes.text: your grandpa's gonna be great in another area code... or something.
jokes.setup: Ashley tries to deliver Will's threat
jokes.punchline: Completely mangles the line, mixing up multiple versions
jokes.explanation: Shows Ashley's nervousness and inability to be threatening. The 'or something' admission of failure is endearing.

jokes.text: And you must be the spawn of Satan.
jokes.setup: Will meets Paula's parents, expected pleasantries
jokes.punchline: Will immediately insults the child to her face
jokes.explanation: Blunt insult that breaks social convention. Economy is good but not particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: Running a little low on cheeks there, bro?
jokes.setup: Carlton says he'll turn the other cheek after being hit
jokes.punchline: Will points out Carlton's face shows he's been hit multiple times
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about the physical reality of Carlton's peaceful philosophy. Good visual humor.

jokes.text: You know, Paula, even the great Martin Luther King... probably drew the line somewhere. And it was probably somewhere around that region you were just aiming.
jokes.setup: Carlton's pacifist philosophy being tested by Paula's aggression
jokes.punchline: Will suggests even MLK would have limits, specifically regarding groin attacks
jokes.explanation: Smart commentary on the limits of non-violence philosophy. The euphemistic reference to anatomy adds family-friendly humor.

jokes.text: I'm quaking in my tube socks.
jokes.setup: Will tries to intimidate Paula
jokes.punchline: Paula sarcastically expresses fear using mundane clothing item
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic retort but the 'tube socks' detail adds character specificity.

jokes.text: $50? How are you playing me? $50? Come on, $20.
jokes.setup: Will offers Paula $5 to leave Ashley alone, Paula counters with $50
jokes.punchline: Will's outraged negotiation showing he's not prepared for serious extortion prices
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's street smarts meeting unexpected high prices. The haggling over extortion is absurd.

jokes.text: It's kind of a rough neighborhood. You want to walk me to my car?
jokes.setup: Will just paid $50 to bully to leave Ashley alone
jokes.punchline: Now acts scared of the same kid he was just intimidating
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's tough guy act crumbling. Good character moment showing his actual cowardice.

jokes.text: Do I need to bring up the culotte incident?
jokes.setup: Hilary establishing her history of fashion interventions
jokes.punchline: Reference to a mysterious past fashion disaster serious enough to be called an 'incident'
jokes.explanation: Good mysterious reference that suggests a traumatic fashion past. Culottes being treated as serious incident is perfect Hilary.

jokes.text: Why don't we just wear Wax Lips?
jokes.setup: Francesca suggests adding a dickey to fix the dress
jokes.punchline: Hillary escalates to completely absurd accessory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Hillary's fashion snobbery. The specific absurdity works well.

jokes.text: Well, I agreed that you should wear white, and I think we all know that's a stretch.
jokes.setup: Francesca says Hillary hasn't agreed with any wedding choices
jokes.punchline: Hillary implies Francesca isn't pure enough for white
jokes.explanation: Classic Hillary cattiness. The implication is mean but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: My neck?
jokes.setup: Ashley claims she wrote an essay, Vivian asks what about
jokes.punchline: Ashley's confused question reveals she didn't actually write anything
jokes.explanation: Shows Ashley's panic as her lie falls apart. The random body part suggests desperation.

jokes.text: Fine, but if I go down, I am taking both of you with me.
jokes.setup: Will whispers advice to Ashley about playing sick
jokes.punchline: Ashley threatens to expose Will and Carlton
jokes.explanation: Shows Ashley has learned some toughness. Good escalation from innocent kid to family blackmailer.

jokes.text: That little extortionist. She promised she wouldn't cash my check until the weekend.
jokes.setup: Carlton's check to Paula bounced
jokes.punchline: Carlton is mad about timing, not the fact he was being extorted
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - completely misses the point of what's wrong with the situation.

jokes.text: Like, picture Mike Tyson in a training bra.
jokes.setup: Philip hasn't seen Paula Hoover
jokes.punchline: Will creates vivid, absurd image of the intimidating bully
jokes.explanation: Vivid, absurd image that effectively communicates Paula's intimidating nature. Good economy and visual.

jokes.text: No. I'll be a total outcast. I'll have to eat lunch with the janitor.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv suggests talking to the principal about the bullying
jokes.punchline: Ashley's dramatic prediction of social exile ending with the specific image of eating with the janitor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that ends with a specific, pathetic image. The janitor detail makes it more vivid than generic social outcast fears.

jokes.text: Let's get ready to rumble!
jokes.setup: Will mentions boxing gloves for Philip
jokes.punchline: Will does boxing announcer impression
jokes.explanation: Predictable boxing reference. Low effort callback to well-known phrase without much originality.

jokes.text: I'm a child psychiatrist.
jokes.setup: Philip asks what kind of doctor Dr. Hoover is
jokes.punchline: Irony that a child psychiatrist has raised such a problematic child
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup for the conflict. The profession makes the family dynamics more absurd.

jokes.text: No sweat, Lurch.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks if the girls can refrain from fighting
jokes.punchline: Paula calls the butler 'Lurch' (Addams Family reference)
jokes.explanation: Basic pop culture reference insult. Shows Paula's disrespectful character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Still got it.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey presumably does something impressive off-screen
jokes.punchline: His understated self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag setup. Geoffrey's dry confidence works with his character but needs the visual component to fully land.

jokes.text: Still got it.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey presumably demonstrates some skill or charm
jokes.punchline: His self-satisfied confirmation of his continued abilities
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat with excellent economy. Geoffrey's vanity comes through in just three words.

jokes.text: Apparently, Ashley has been quite ruthless with her.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the girls not getting along
jokes.punchline: Parents blame victim Ashley instead of bully Paula
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing parents' obliviousness. Sets up the conflict between families.

jokes.text: She's probably just self-conscious about that tick. I'd be happy to prescribe something.
jokes.setup: Reference to Will's earlier 'tick' advice
jokes.punchline: Doctor wants to medicate Ashley for Will's fake fighting technique
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier scene. Shows doctor's quick-to-medicate tendency.

jokes.text: Wow. If I'd known, I never would have beat up his sister.
jokes.setup: Paula learns Brad Nelson likes Ashley
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions beating up Brad's sister too
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. Shows Paula's casual violence while being oddly considerate.

jokes.text: Sure you don't want to get a couple sucker punches in there?
jokes.setup: Ashley and Paula have made peace
jokes.punchline: Will still wants violence despite resolution
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's disappointment at peaceful resolution. Character-appropriate bloodthirstiness.

jokes.text: Sure you don't want to get a couple sucker punches in there?
jokes.setup: Ashley and Paula have made peace
jokes.punchline: Will still wants to encourage violence even after the problem is solved
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Will's inability to let go of conflict even when resolved. The suggestion is appropriately inappropriate.

jokes.text: Too many big words?
jokes.setup: Vivian questions the psychological diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Hoover condescendingly assumes Vivian doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Sets up Vivian's angry response. Basic condescending humor but serves story function.

jokes.text: Good school. I was thinking of going there if my scholarship... to Princeton, Yale, Wharton, and Talladega Tech had fallen through.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hoover mentions Penn State
jokes.punchline: Will lists prestigious schools then adds NASCAR technical college
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with unexpected ending. The Talladega reference undercuts the bragging nicely.

jokes.text: Impressive. You must have been quite an athlete in your thinner days.
jokes.setup: Will brags about athletic scholarships
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hoover insults Will's current weight
jokes.explanation: Basic insult humor. Not particularly clever but escalates the conflict.

jokes.text: Speaking as a doctor... I think your daughter ought to be heavily sedated... and immediately institutionalized.
jokes.setup: The parents' argument escalating into professional insults
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hoover's extreme professional recommendation for Ashley
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the professional fight. The extreme diagnosis for innocent Ashley shows how petty the adults have become.

jokes.text: I think you must have her confused with your mama.
jokes.setup: Philip makes elaborate insult about Paula's appearance
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hoover responds with simple 'your mama' comeback
jokes.explanation: Generic 'your mama' joke. Low effort comedy that contrasts with Philip's elaborate insult.

jokes.text: I think you must have her confused with your mama.
jokes.setup: Phil's elaborate insult about Paula
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hoover's juvenile 'your mama' comeback
jokes.explanation: Weak comeback that shows how the 'professional' has devolved to playground insults. The contrast with Phil's eloquence makes it effective in context.

jokes.text: That's it, Uncle Phil! You're grounded!
jokes.setup: Philip and Dr. Hoover are fighting
jokes.punchline: Will treats Philip like a misbehaving child
jokes.explanation: Great role reversal joke with perfect economy. Shows Will taking adult responsibility in absurd situation.

jokes.text: And to think I was gracious enough to let her invite her hayseed grandmother.
jokes.setup: Hilary is upset about Francesca eloping after all her dress work
jokes.punchline: Her focus on the irrelevant grandmother invitation shows her misplaced priorities
jokes.explanation: Good Hilary moment showing her self-centeredness. The 'hayseed grandmother' detail shows her snobbery even in defeat.

jokes.text: It's like: My broski! Whatever you want to do... broham, on the left or the right we can do that all night. But all that old yackety-yak, jawbone, you kicking in the middle, man... you can stop that, 'cause I ain't down with that. You see what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Will explains his neighborhood's sacred code
jokes.punchline: Delivers complete nonsense presented as profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment. Will creates elaborate street code that means nothing but sounds profound.

jokes.text: Me, neither. That's why it's so sacred.
jokes.setup: Carlton and Philip don't understand Will's 'code'
jokes.punchline: Will admits he doesn't understand it either, but that makes it sacred
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that doubles down on the absurdity. Great self-aware humor with excellent economy.

jokes.text: Everything that feels good is wrong. Everything. Do you understand me?
jokes.setup: Philip's moral lesson about feeling good being wrong
jokes.punchline: Takes the lesson to absurd extreme, forbidding all pleasure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Philip's parental overcorrection. The extremity makes it funny.
